article_title: Perovskite Solar Cells Hit Two Milestones in April: A Certified Efficiency Record and a Stability Breakthrough

content_preview.title: Perovskite Solar Cells Hit Two Milestones in April: A Certified Efficiency Record and a Stability Breakthrough
content_preview.summary: A Nature Energy paper from Nanchang University achieves 26.61% certified efficiency with cesium doping, while a private Chinese cell surpasses all single-junction silicon cells at 27.98%.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

April 2026 produced two significant advances in perovskite solar technology within the span of a few weeks. Researchers at Nanchang University in China published a peer-reviewed technique in *Nature Energy* that achieved a certified efficiency of 26.61% while also demonstrating exceptional thermal durability. Days earlier, a separately reported Chinese cell reached 27.98% efficiency — surpassing all single-junction silicon solar cells ever tested in a laboratory setting for the firs...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
summary: Three efficiency figures attributed to GreenFuelJournal do not match that source: silicon cap 29% (source says ~32%), perovskite ceiling 31% (not in source), commercial modules 20-22% (source says 24.5-28.6%).

findings_appended.message: Three efficiency figures attributed to GreenFuelJournal do not match source content
findings_appended.details: The sentence 'Single-junction silicon cells are generally considered to cap out near 29%, while perovskite's theoretical ceiling is around 31%, [as noted by industry analysts tracking the field]' links to GreenFuelJournal. WebFetch of GreenFuelJournal shows: (1) silicon Shockley-Queisser limit cited as '~32%', not 29%; (2) perovskite '31%' ceiling not mentioned at all. Additionally, 'commercial-scale modules typically measure 20–22%' attributed to GreenFuelJournal, but source says '24.5–28.6% efficiency range' for commercial modules. Fix: remove the inline citation and present these as general industry knowledge without a source link, OR cite a source that actually contains these figures.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 864-word News piece covering three distinct April 2026 perovskite advances. Good structure with separate sections for each development. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately flags the CGTN 27.98% claim as unverified and uncertified. Journalist correctly notes CGTN did not name the developer.
editor_notes.source_verification: TechXplore (Nanchang University): fetched — cesium 4-(diphenylphosphino)benzoate ✓, two-step fabrication ✓, 26.91% uncertified ✓, 26.61% certified ✓, alpha-phase stabilization ✓, 95% retention after 1500h at 85°C ISOS-L-2 ✓, Nature Energy ✓. CGTN: fetched — 27.98% ✓, first time outperforms single-junction silicon ✓, April 3 ✓, no institution named ✓ (article correctly caveats this). SolarQuarter (Halocell/Sofab Inks): fetched — Halocell Energy + Sofab Inks ✓, metal oxide nanoparticle inks ✓, 100% normalized efficiency after 1300h ✓, 1000 lux / 85% humidity / 65°C ✓, ~20% loss for conventional after 1000h ✓, roll-to-roll ✓, IoT/drones/space ✓. GreenFuelJournal: fetched — Oxford PV shipping 2024 ✓, mass production 2027-2028 ✓, market $1.68-7.02B by 2030 ✓; BUT silicon 29% cap NOT in source (source says ~32%), perovskite 31% NOT in source, commercial 20-22% NOT in source (source says 24.5-28.6%) ✗.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core news content fully verified: TechXplore claims ✓, CGTN 27.98% ✓, SolarQuarter stability data ✓. GreenFuelJournal confirms market and timeline figures. Three efficiency figures attributed to GreenFuelJournal are not in that source and conflict with what the source actually states.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriate caveats around the uncertified CGTN claim and the lead toxicity/regulatory issue.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of April 2026 perovskite advances.
concerns: Silicon ~29%, perovskite ~31%, and commercial module 20-22% efficiency figures attributed to GreenFuelJournal but not confirmed in that source (source gives different figures)
recommendations: Remove inline citation to GreenFuelJournal from the efficiency ceiling paragraph and present silicon/perovskite limits as general knowledge, or find a source that actually cites these figures
Fix commercial module efficiency range to match GreenFuelJournal (24.5-28.6%) or attribute to a source that supports 20-22%
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong article with well-verified core news content. Three efficiency figures in the context section are attributed to a source that doesn't contain them. Fixable with one paragraph edit.
